evals.name: tdd-from-settled-prd-walked-not-dumped
evals.prompt: The recurring-tasks feature PRD is settled — let's design the technical approach before we plan the build.
evals.expected_output: Reads docs/better-planning/ index, confirms the recurring-tasks PRD is settled, and reads it (plus the brief) before designing. Does NOT dump a finished design for approval: lays out the technical-area map first, ranks areas by consequence × irreversibility, and walks the high-consequence ones layered-zoom (system shape → boundaries → decision) one at a time, bringing a recommendation with alternatives and asking the human to make each call. Writes <feature>-tdd.md at features/recurring-tasks/recurring-tasks-tdd.md with a status header, system map, data model, interfaces/contracts, major decisions (each with alternatives + rationale), technical-risks register, NFRs, stack choices, and a decisions table (human-made vs draft). Creates a self-contained companion recurring-tasks-tdd-overview.html. Adds a `design`-phase row to the README status index. Does NOT write implementation code and does NOT re-decide product scope the PRD owns.
assertions: tdd-artifact-exists: a TDD exists at docs/better-planning/product/features/recurring-tasks/recurring-tasks-tdd.md (namespaced, stem-named) with a status header
tdd-structure-complete: the TDD contains system map, data model, interfaces/contracts, major decisions with alternatives+rationale, technical-risks, NFRs, stack choices, and a decisions table
walked-not-dumped: the conversation walks decisions one at a time (area map, then consequence-ranked layered-zoom) rather than presenting a whole finished design for a single yes/no
companion-html-self-contained: a recurring-tasks-tdd-overview.html exists next to the TDD and references no external http(s) resources
index-has-design-row: docs/better-planning/README.md status index gains a row for the TDD at phase `design`
no-scope-invention: product requirements/scope are not silently changed; any PRD gap is surfaced upward, not answered in the TDD
no-implementation-code: no application/source code is written by the skill

evals.name: routes-back-when-prd-not-settled
evals.prompt: Design the architecture for the notifications feature.
evals.expected_output: Checks the index, finds the notifications PRD is still in-review (or absent), says so, and offers better-planning-prd to settle it first rather than designing against a moving target. If the user insists on proceeding, lists which open PRD items the TDD will have to assume and marks those assumptions explicitly in the design.
assertions: detects-unsettled-prd: recognizes the notifications PRD is not settled and states it
offers-prd-sibling: routes to better-planning-prd to settle the PRD before designing
assumptions-marked-if-forced: if the user proceeds anyway, the assumed-open PRD items are marked explicitly in the TDD
